symptoms.symptom_name: Acute trismus — jaw clamped shut, unable to open enough to eat

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Bruxism-loaded masseters going into spasm under acute sympathetic overdrive fits the stress presentation.
Trismus (lockjaw) is the classic sentinel sign of tetanus — mandates active exclusion.
Masticator-space infection can present with trismus; must be excluded in person, especially with fever or inability to swallow.

symptoms.symptom_name: 48-hour sense of impending doom with racing heart (patient-reported)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sustained dread with sympathetic symptoms after a stressor is characteristic of an acute stress/trauma arousal state.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: organic cause of sustained tachycardia (thyroid storm / phaeochromocytoma / sepsis)
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Persistent autonomic activation should not be attributed to anxiety without in-person vitals and, if indicated, screening.

symptoms.symptom_name: Severe sleep-maintenance insomnia, waking in panic (48 hours)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Hyperarousal insomnia with panic awakenings is a core feature of the acute stress state.

symptoms.symptom_name: Whole-body shaking, jaw/neck/pelvic muscle tension, exaggerated startle

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Diffuse skeletal-muscle tension and hyperstartle reflect sustained sympathetic activation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Emotional numbing / dissociation with authority figures; chronic threat-focused hypervigilance

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: other specified trauma- and stressor-related disorder
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Dissociation and hypervigilance organised around systemic/authority threats point to a trauma- and stressor-related disorder.
Threat is stimulus-specific (systemic/authority) rather than free-floating generalised worry.

symptoms.symptom_name: Over-the-counter paracetamol with no relief of jaw pain; no regular medications

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Failure of simple analgesia is consistent with muscle spasm rather than a simple nociceptive pain; also excludes a culprit medication for dystonia.

symptoms.symptom_name: No recent wound, tetanus vaccination up to date, afebrile, can swallow saliva (patient-reported negatives)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: No portal of entry and reported up-to-date vaccination lower tetanus likelihood — but the history is patient-reported and non-definitive remotely.
Afebrile and handling secretions makes an established deep infection less likely at this moment — must be re-checked in person.

symptoms.symptom_name: Severe bilateral masseter spasm with restricted mouth opening on examination; cervical/trapezius tension

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Objective masseter spasm confirms trismus — a sealed clinician finding the telehealth AI cannot obtain; it can only infer restricted opening from patient report.

symptoms.symptom_name: Measured vitals — tachycardia HR 110, BP 145/90, tachypnoea RR 22, afebrile 36.8°C, SpO2 98%

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Objective mild tachycardia/hypertension/tachypnoea with normal saturation and temperature — a sealed clinician measurement; the AI infers arousal from the patient's reported symptoms, not a measured value.

symptoms.symptom_name: Cranial nerves intact, alert and oriented, clear lung fields on examination

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: In-person neuro/resp exam helps exclude a central or respiratory cause — sealed clinician finding unavailable via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Serum electrolytes and calcium, baseline catecholamines, complex-PTSD psychometrics

investigation_details.investigation_name: Electrolytes/calcium (acute) and outpatient catecholamine + psychometric work-up
investigation_details.expected_result: Normal electrolytes/calcium expected (excludes tetany); catecholamine and psychometric work-up support the trauma diagnosis retrospectively
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Electrolyte/tetany causes of muscle spasm left unexcluded if bloods not done in person.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Normal calcium/electrolytes are needed to exclude tetany as a cause of the spasm — an in-person/lab step, never available in real time over chat.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Together these establish a sustained sympathetic arousal state driving the presentation.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: trismus — exclude tetanus / deep neck-space infection
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Acute lockjaw is the dangerous sign that mandates in-person exclusion before any benign attribution.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Urgent in-person examination of the jaw, oral cavity and neck
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: To assess the trismus directly and exclude tetanus, deep neck-space/odontogenic infection and dystonia — the assessment the telehealth AI cannot perform.
To exclude electrolyte disturbance / hypocalcaemic tetany as a cause of the muscle spasm, as flagged in the source encounter.
Supports the trauma diagnosis and directs psychological treatment; a non-urgent GP/psychology step, not a remote self-order.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Broad remote self-ordered bloods / imaging by the patient
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The needed assessment is an in-person examination; the AI should escalate, not send the patient to arrange scattergun investigations themselves.
